description: Merged handbook and literature sources — equal-level domain authorities

sources.title: Low C-rate cycle / Slow (dis)charge curve
sources.handbook_title: Test methods for improved battery cell understanding
sources.citation_ieee: [M1] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Low C-rate cycle / Slow (dis)charge curve," White Paper, 2018, p. 24.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [M1] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Low C-rate cycle / Slow (dis)charge curve," White Paper, 2018, p. 24.
ingestion_notes: Treat this chapter as the core planning reference for slow-curve and SOC-OCV style tests.
Use literature only to complement theory or interpretation; do not silently override the handbook procedure.

sources.title: Capacity test
sources.handbook_title: Test methods for improved battery cell understanding
sources.citation_ieee: [M2] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Capacity test," White Paper, 2018, pp. 18-23.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [M2] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Capacity test," White Paper, 2018, pp. 18-23.
ingestion_notes: Treat this chapter as the core planning reference for capacity and rate-capability test drafting.
Literature can explain why one matrix is useful, but the chapter remains the default executable reference.

sources.title: Pulse test
sources.handbook_title: Test methods for improved battery cell understanding
sources.citation_ieee: [M3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Pulse test," White Paper, 2018, pp. 31-37.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [M3] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Pulse test," White Paper, 2018, pp. 31-37.
ingestion_notes: Treat this chapter as the core planning reference for HPPC and pulse-power style test design.
Use complementary literature for theory or interpretation, not to silently change the pulse sequence.

sources.title: Cycle life test
sources.handbook_title: Test methods for improved battery cell understanding
sources.citation_ieee: [M4] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Cycle life test," White Paper, 2018, pp. 53-54.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [M4] G. Mulder, H. Popp, W. Kohs, K. Trad, K. de Craemer, W. Porcher, M. Montaru, M. A. Martinez, A. Marongiu, M. Oyarbide et al., "Test methods for improved battery cell understanding: Cycle life test," White Paper, 2018, pp. 53-54.
ingestion_notes: Treat this chapter as the core planning reference for cycle-life test framing and review gates.
Complementary literature is especially useful for DOE choice and pack-specific ageing rationale, but it does not replace the handbook as the core reference.

sources.title: Design of experiments applied to lithium-ion batteries: A literature review
sources.citation_ieee: [1] L. A. Roman-Ramirez and J. Marco, "Design of experiments applied to lithium-ion batteries: A literature review," Applied Energy, vol. 320, art. no. 119305, 2022.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [[1] L. A. Roman-Ramirez and J. Marco, "Design of experiments applied to lithium-ion batteries: A literature review," Applied Energy, vol. 320, art. no. 119305, 2022.](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306261922006596#s0005)
ingestion_notes: Summarized from a user-supplied PDF; the repository stores rephrased notes plus page-level citations instead of the raw PDF.
Use this review as a decision aid for DOE choice, not as a replacement for lab-specific safety limits, sample budgets, or equipment constraints.

sources.title: Design of Experiments
authors: Institute of Statistics, Graz University of Technology
sources.citation_ieee: [2] Institute of Statistics, Graz University of Technology, "Design of Experiments," course notes. [Online]. Available: https://www.stat.tugraz.at/courses/files/DoE.pdf
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [[2] Institute of Statistics, Graz University of Technology, "Design of Experiments," course notes. [Online]. Available: https://www.stat.tugraz.at/courses/files/DoE.pdf](https://www.stat.tugraz.at/courses/files/DoE.pdf)
ingestion_notes: Summarized from a user-supplied PDF; the repository stores rephrased notes plus page-level citations instead of the raw PDF.
Use this source as a DOE theory foundation for ANOVA, randomization, blocking, factorial effects, and confounding.

For battery-specific design-family choice or charging, thermal, and parameter-identification guidance, pair it with a battery-domain source such as Roman-Ramirez and Marco (2022) instead of using it alone.

sources.title: Degradation in parallel-connected lithium-ion battery packs under thermal gradients
sources.citation_ieee: [3] M. Naylor Marlow, J. Chen, and B. Wu, "Degradation in parallel-connected lithium-ion battery packs under thermal gradients," Communications Engineering, vol. 3, no. 1, p. 2, 2024.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [[3] M. Naylor Marlow, J. Chen, and B. Wu, "Degradation in parallel-connected lithium-ion battery packs under thermal gradients," Communications Engineering, vol. 3, no. 1, p. 2, 2024.](https://www.nature.com/articles/s44172-023-00153-5)
ingestion_notes: Summarized from a user-supplied main article PDF plus supplementary-information PDF; the repository stores rephrased notes plus page-level citations instead of the raw PDFs.
Use this source for battery-domain guidance on parallel-pack ageing, thermal-gradient effects, current sharing, and multimodal diagnostics rather than for generic DOE-family choice.
Pair it with Roman-Ramirez and Marco (2022) when the user needs both pack-level battery evidence and a more formal DOE design rationale.

sources.title: A comparison of methodologies for the non-invasive characterisation of commercial Li-ion cells
sources.publication: Progress in Energy and Combustion Science
sources.citation_ieee: [4] A. Barai, K. Uddin, M. Dubarry, L. Somerville, A. McGordon, P. Jennings, and I. Bloom, "A comparison of methodologies for the non-invasive characterisation of commercial Li-ion cells," Progress in Energy and Combustion Science, vol. 72, pp. 1-31, 2019.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [[4] A. Barai, K. Uddin, M. Dubarry, L. Somerville, A. McGordon, P. Jennings, and I. Bloom, "A comparison of methodologies for the non-invasive characterisation of commercial Li-ion cells," Progress in Energy and Combustion Science, vol. 72, pp. 1-31, 2019.](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0360128518300996)
ingestion_notes: Summarized from a user-supplied PDF; the repository stores rephrased notes plus page-level citations instead of the raw PDF.
Use this review as the primary theory source for non-invasive electrical characterisation methods and method-selection guidance.
Pair it with more specific chemistry- or workflow-oriented sources when the user asks about one exact cell type, pack topology, or case-study experiment.

sources.title: Study of the insertion reaction by impedance - Application to the characterization of batteries
sources.citation_ieee: [5] B. L., "Study of the insertion reaction by impedance - Application to the characterization of batteries," technical slides, 2012.
sources.linked_reference_markdown: [5] B. L., "Study of the insertion reaction by impedance - Application to the characterization of batteries," technical slides, 2012.
ingestion_notes: Summarized from a user-supplied slide deck; the repository stores rephrased notes plus page-level citations instead of the raw PDF.
Use this source as a supporting theory note for insertion-reaction impedance, Warburg behaviour, bounded diffusion, and adsorption-coupled EIS interpretation.
Because this is a technical slide deck rather than a peer-reviewed paper, pair it with a stronger review source such as Barai et al. (2019) when giving general battery-EIS guidance.
